metadata.official_name: The University of Texas at Austin

strategic_profile.executive_summary: The University of Texas at Austin is a premier public research university and the flagship institution of the University of Texas System [cite: 1, 2]. Renowned for top-tier programs in engineering, business, and computer science, the university blends rigorous academics with a vibrant, community-oriented campus life [cite: 3, 4]. Situated in a booming tech hub, UT Austin offers massive scale, deep resources, and a fiercely competitive admissions environment, particularly for out-of-state students and specific impacted majors [cite: 5, 6].

analyst_takeaways.insight: State law mandates that 90% of the freshman class must be Texas residents, and approximately 75% of those spots are filled via automatic admission (currently the top 6%, tightening to top 5% for 2026) [cite: 5, 7, 8].
analyst_takeaways.implication: Out-of-state and international applicants face brutal selectivity (~7.8% - 10% acceptance rate), akin to elite private universities [cite: 7, 9].

analyst_takeaways.insight: Admissions are highly major-dependent. Guaranteed admission to the university does not guarantee admission to impacted programs like Computer Science or Business [cite: 10].
analyst_takeaways.implication: Applicants must strategize their major selection carefully; internal transfers into these highly coveted programs are statistically unlikely for most students [cite: 10, 11].

analyst_takeaways.insight: Median earnings ten years post-enrollment stand at a robust $79,115, with highly ranked programs like Petroleum Engineering driving starting salaries over $119,000 [cite: 12, 13].
analyst_takeaways.implication: UT Austin offers a high return on investment, particularly for in-state students benefiting from heavily subsidized tuition [cite: 13, 14].

analyst_takeaways.insight: The campus hosts over 53,000 students and features a highly spirited environment anchored by Longhorn sports, Greek life, and over 1,000 student organizations [cite: 1, 6].
analyst_takeaways.implication: Students looking for a massive, high-energy, traditional college experience in a culturally rich urban center will thrive here, though it may be overwhelming for those seeking a small-school feel [cite: 3, 6].

campus_dynamics.social_environment: Grounded in deep traditions, UT Austin offers a dynamic and spirited social scene heavily influenced by major college athletics, the local Austin music and food culture, and a massive array of student clubs [cite: 3, 6].
campus_dynamics.transportation_impact: Set in the urban core of Austin, Texas, the campus provides exceptional access to state government and tech industry internships, though students note that the sheer size of the campus and Austin's traffic can make commuting and parking challenging [cite: 6, 15].
campus_dynamics.research_impact: As a Tier-1 research institution with over $1.06 billion in annual research expenditures, the university boasts world-class laboratories and provides extensive opportunities for undergraduate research across disciplines [cite: 1, 16].

current_status.test_policy_details: As of the 2025-2026 application cycle, UT Austin is no longer test-optional. Standardized SAT or ACT scores are required for admission [cite: 7, 17].

longitudinal_trends.notes: Waitlist data not publicly disclosed in recent Common Data Sets [cite: 18, 19].

gpa.notes: UT Austin does not publish GPA distributions. However, roughly 75% of the freshman class is filled through automatic admission of the top 6% of Texas high school graduates [cite: 5, 22].

testing.policy_note: Test-optional policy has ended; 56% submitted SAT scores when it was optional [cite: 7, 16].

colleges.name: Red McCombs School of Business
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Ideal for students with strong quantitative skills, leadership experience in organizations like DECA, and a clear vision for business application [cite: 10, 23].

majors.name: Business Administration
majors.special_requirements: Internal transfers must attend information sessions and submit an essay detailing their strategic fit for business [cite: 23, 24].
notable_professors: John N. Doggett (Global Business and Entrepreneurship)
majors.notes: Internal transfer is highly competitive. Many 4.0 students are denied [cite: 23, 25, 26, 27].

colleges.name: College of Natural Sciences
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Students with rigorous STEM backgrounds and advanced math/science coursework [cite: 11, 28].

majors.name: Computer Science
majors.special_requirements: External transfer rate is around 1-2%. Internal transfer requires rigorous academic performance and an essay, but spaces are extremely limited [cite: 9, 11, 29].
majors.notes: Highly prestigious and competitive program. Turing Scholars honors track is extremely selective [cite: 30, 31, 32].

majors.name: Biology
majors.notes: One of the most popular majors at UT Austin [cite: 9, 33].

colleges.name: Cockrell School of Engineering
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Students with top-tier math and physics profiles seeking rigorous applied science training [cite: 10, 34].
colleges.student_archetype: Innovative, mathematically gifted builder and problem solver.

majors.name: Aerospace Engineering
majors.notes: Students choose between atmospheric flight and space flight tracks [cite: 35, 36, 37].

application_deadlines.notes: Early Action deadline; decisions released by January 15 [cite: 38].
Regular Decision deadline; decisions released by February 15 [cite: 38].

supplemental_requirements.details: One major essay and several short-answer prompts are required [cite: 17].
Certain programs like Fine Arts or Architecture may require additional materials [cite: 17].

holistic_factors.specific_differentiators: Admission is heavily determined by major choice and Texas state residency class-rank mandates [cite: 10, 39, 40].

major_selection_tactics: Apply to related but less impacted majors if your profile isn't perfect for top programs. For example, apply to Advertising in the Moody College instead of Marketing in McCombs [cite: 41].
Consider Computational Engineering or Electrical and Computer Engineering instead of pure Computer Science [cite: 28].
Do not list an impacted major as your first choice if your primary goal is just to attend UT Austin, as secondary choices are rarely granted for STEM majors [cite: 10, 11].
college_ranking_tactics: For Texas residents, aim to secure a spot in the top 6% (or 5%) of the class to guarantee university admission, though major placement remains holistic [cite: 8, 10].
application_strategy.alternate_major_strategy: The Coordinated Admission Program (CAP) allows Texas residents not admitted initially to attend another UT System school for a year, guaranteeing transfer to the College of Liberal Arts at UT Austin upon meeting GPA requirements (3.2+). Alternatively, students can apply undeclared to Liberal Arts and attempt a highly competitive internal transfer later [cite: 42, 43].

financials.tuition_model: In-state tuition is heavily subsidized; Longhorn Fixed Tuition program locks in rates for four years [cite: 3].

scholarships.name: Forty Acres Scholars Program
scholarships.benefits: Covers full tuition, living stipend, book stipend, and funding for enrichment activities like study abroad. Includes access to a tight-knit cohort and exclusive networking [cite: 44, 45, 46].

ap_policy.general_rule: Awards credit for at least one course for most AP exams with a score of 3 or higher [cite: 47].
ap_policy.usage: Can be used for placement into specific courses or to earn degree credit [cite: 47, 48].

ib_policy.general_rule: Awards credit based on IB Certificate/Course exams or full IB Diploma status [cite: 47].

transfer_articulation.restrictions: Credit by examination does not count as credit earned in residence and does not impact institutional GPA [cite: 48].

what_it_takes: In-state applicants effectively need top 6% ranking to secure a spot [cite: 7].
Out-of-state applicants need elite academic profiles (75th percentile +) akin to Ivy League standards [cite: 8, 10].
Compelling alignment with the requested major via essays and coursework [cite: 28].
common_activities: DECA / Business Professionals of America (for Business)
essay_tips: Clearly articulate 'Why this major' and connect it to your background and UT's specific resources [cite: 28].
Showcase leadership and a commitment to using your education for societal benefit (aligning with 'What Starts Here Changes the World') [cite: 2, 45].
red_flags: Applying to highly competitive majors (CS, McCombs) as an out-of-state student without exceptional stats and a backup plan [cite: 11, 50].
Submitting generic essays that do not explain the specific fit for the requested major [cite: 51].
insights: Internal transfers into top majors (CS, Business) are notoriously difficult; it is advised to apply directly to the desired major rather than trying to game the system [cite: 11, 24].
